title: Crédit Agricole Egypt (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Crédit Agricole Egypt in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Crédit Agricole Egypt?
faq.answer: Crédit Agricole Egypt is a universal bank and the only French banking institution operating in Egypt. As a subsidiary of the Crédit Agricole Group, one of Europe's largest financial institutions, it serves both corporate and retail customers across the country. The bank operates approximately 78 branches and serves over 356,000 customers with a workforce of around 2,300 employees. Crédit Agricole Egypt is the only commercial bank listed on the Cairo Stock Exchange, trading under the ticker symbol CIEB.CA. It is also part of the EGX/ESG S&P sustainability index, demonstrating its commitment to responsible banking practices.

faq.question: When was Crédit Agricole Egypt founded?
faq.answer: Crédit Agricole Egypt was established in 2006 through a series of successive mergers that brought together four major financial institutions: Banque Indosuez, Crédit Commercial de France (CCF), Crédit Lyonnais, and Egyptian American Bank (EAB). This strategic consolidation marked the Crédit Agricole Group's formal entry into the Egyptian market. Originally focused primarily on corporate banking, the bank has since evolved into a universal banking institution, with retail banking now generating more than 40% of its net banking income.

faq.question: Where is Crédit Agricole Egypt headquartered?
faq.answer: Crédit Agricole Egypt is headquartered in Zamalek, Cairo, with its main office located at 4/6 Hassan Sabri Street. The bank recently developed a state-of-the-art head office complex in New Cairo, specifically in the Fifth Settlement area near Choueifat, designed to accommodate up to 1,500 employees. This modern headquarters complex was designed to achieve LEED Platinum certification, reflecting the bank's commitment to sustainability and environmental responsibility.

faq.question: Where does Crédit Agricole Egypt operate?
faq.answer: Crédit Agricole Egypt maintains a nationwide presence across Egypt with approximately 78 branches strategically distributed in high population density and business potential areas. Major operational hubs include: • Cairo (30 branches): Including Nasr City, Heliopolis, Maadi, New Cairo, and Zamalek • Alexandria (13 branches): Including Semouha, Sporting, and coastal areas • Giza (14 branches): Including Mohandeseen, 6th of October City, and Haram • Sharm El Sheikh (3 branches): Including Naama Bay and Nabq Bay • Other governorates: Including Aswan, Asyut, Benisuef, Damietta, Mansoura, Hurghada, Ismailia, Luxor, Port Said, and Qena The bank has also equipped 20 branches to specifically serve customers with disabilities, exceeding Central Bank of Egypt requirements.

faq.question: What services does Crédit Agricole Egypt offer?
faq.answer: Crédit Agricole Egypt provides a comprehensive range of financial services across multiple business lines: Retail Banking: Current accounts, savings accounts, fixed deposits, personal loans, car loans, mortgages, debit and credit cards, and specialized accounts for people with disabilities. The bank particularly targets affluent customers with premium banking services. Corporate Banking: Project finance, trade finance, syndicated loans, working capital financing, cash management services, treasury operations, foreign exchange solutions, and capital markets products. The bank serves multi-national companies, large corporates, and mid-cap businesses. SME Banking: Tailored financing solutions for small and medium-sized enterprises, including streamlined lending workflows and specialized digital services. Private Banking (Banque Privée): Wealth management and personalized services for high-net-worth individuals, leveraging the Crédit Agricole Group's global private banking expertise. Digital Banking Services: The bank offers cutting-edge digital platforms including banki Mobile (awarded "Most Innovative Retail Banking App"), banki Wallet (a mobile wallet supporting Fawry network transactions), online banking, tablet banking, telephone banking (IVR), and SMS alert services. In Q1 2025, the bank processed 4.5 million digital transactions with over 2.5 million app logins. Sustainable Finance: Green lending programs through the EBRD-backed GEFF II framework, supporting energy-efficient and renewable technology investments.

faq.question: What makes Crédit Agricole Egypt unique?
faq.answer: Only French Bank in Egypt: Crédit Agricole Egypt holds the distinction of being the sole French banking institution operating in the Egyptian market, bringing European banking expertise and standards to Egypt. Digital Innovation Leadership: The bank has won numerous awards for digital excellence, including "Best Digital Transformation Programme," "Digital Banking Provider of the Year," and "Most Innovative Retail Banking App" in 2023 and 2024. It was the first bank in Egypt to offer a true omni-channel experience across mobile, tablet, and desktop platforms. Cairo Stock Exchange Listing: As the only commercial bank listed on the Cairo Stock Exchange, it demonstrates exceptional transparency and corporate governance standards. Strong Financial Fundamentals: The bank maintains remarkably low non-performing loan (NPL) ratios around 2% with strong coverage and capital adequacy. It has successfully navigated major political crises (2011 and 2013) and currency devaluations (2016 and 2024) while maintaining profitability levels exceeding 40% RONE (Return on Net Equity). Group Synergies: The bank leverages extensive synergies within the Crédit Agricole Group, particularly with Crédit Agricole Corporate and Investment Bank (CACIB), LCL, and Regional Banks to serve multinational corporate clients. Sustainability Commitment: Active participation in sustainable finance initiatives, including membership in the United Nations Global Compact (UNGC) and United Nations Environment Programme Finance Initiative (UNEP FI), with alignment to Egypt Vision 2030. Advanced Digital Adoption: Nearly 99% of domestic transfers are completed digitally, with over 48% of corporate and SME clients digitally active. The bank's Banki Commerce platform facilitates e-commerce and governmental payments.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Crédit Agricole Egypt?
faq.answer: Crédit Agricole Egypt is a universal bank operating under a commercial banking license issued by the Central Bank of Egypt. The bank transformed from its original corporate banking focus to adopt a universal banking model that serves the complete spectrum of financial services. This model enables it to provide integrated financial solutions across retail banking, corporate banking, investment banking, wealth management, and specialized financial services under a single institution. As a universal bank, it combines the functions of both a retail bank (serving individual customers and SMEs) and an investment/corporate bank (serving large corporations and providing capital markets services). This comprehensive approach allows Crédit Agricole Egypt to offer "a whole bank just for you" to meet diverse customer needs across all segments of the Egyptian economy.
